response: The award Alex Rodriguez won two of is the Commissioner's Historic Achievement Award, which is presented by the Commissioner of Baseball to a group or person who has made a "major impact on the sport" of baseball. This award has been presented fourteen times by Commissioner Bud Selig: eleven times to players, once to a team, and twice to a non-player. The most recent recipient is Derek Jeter (2014), who was honored in the final year of his career for being "one of the most accomplished shortstops of all-time." Alex Rodriguez was the first to receive the award for his parts in the 1998 MLB home run record chase.
